wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the my-Blob blog management system. Explain its purpose as a full-stack blog platform with admin panel and public interface, architecture overview, and relationship between backend (Spring Boot) and frontend (Vue.js) components. Document the technology stack including Java 17+, Spring Boot 3.2.5, MyBatis, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, and JWT authentication. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the admin workflow and public blog browsing. Document the system's key features including content management, file upload, search functionality, and responsive design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the my-Blob blog management system. Include prerequisites (Java 17+, Node.js, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch), step-by-step installation instructions, database setup with schema and initial data, environment configuration, and running both backend and frontend applications. Document the development server startup process, port configurations, and initial admin account setup. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Include practical examples of accessing the admin panel and public blog interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Spring Boot Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Spring Boot application structure and startup configuration. Explain the @SpringBootApplication annotation, component scanning configuration, MyBatis mapper scanning setup, and caching enablement. Document the application entry point, Maven dependencies, and project structure organization. Include details about Spring Boot auto-configuration, application properties loading, and environment-specific configurations. Provide practical examples of how the application initializes and starts up, including component registration and bean creation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for admin authentication endpoints. Document the POST /api/admin/login endpoint including request body schema with username and password fields, successful response containing JWT token, and error responses for invalid credentials. Include authentication flow explanation, token generation process, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of login requests and responses with curl commands. Document token expiration, refresh mechanisms, and integration with JWT interceptor middleware.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vue.js Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Vue.js application structure and initialization. Detail the main.js entry point configuration including Vue app creation, plugin registration (Pinia, Router), and global imports. Explain the App.vue root component structure and its role in the application hierarchy. Document the Vite build configuration, development server setup, and production build process. Include package.json dependencies, script configurations, and environment setup. Address application lifecycle, global CSS imports, and third-party library integration patterns. Provide practical examples of extending the application structure and adding new plugins.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Layout and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the admin layout component and navigation system. Describe the responsive sidebar layout, mobile-friendly navigation toggle, and dark theme implementation. Document the component structure including sidebar navigation items, topbar functionality, and menu state management. Explain the Vue 3 Composition API implementation with reactive state, router integration, and authentication-driven navigation. Include CSS styling patterns for the glass-morphism design, responsive breakpoints, and component styling approach. Address the mobile-first design strategy, sidebar positioning, and navigation accessibility features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Controller Layer Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the controller layer implementation in the Spring Boot application. Detail the AdminController and PublicController classes, explaining their RESTful endpoint design, HTTP method mappings, and request/response handling patterns. Document the controller responsibilities including request validation, parameter binding, exception handling, and response formatting. Explain the integration with WebConfig for CORS configuration and JwtInterceptor for authentication middleware. Include examples of endpoint implementations, parameter handling, file upload processing, and error response patterns. Address controller best practices, security considerations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Service - Authentication and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Admin Service responsible for authentication and authorization functionality. Detail the admin user management operations including password encoding with BCrypt, username lookup, and automatic admin account creation. Explain the service's role in the authentication flow, password validation methods, and security considerations. Document the integration with AdminMapper for database operations, the BCryptPasswordEncoder usage for secure password hashing, and the createAdminIfNotExists method for initial setup. Include examples of admin login validation, password comparison, and security best practices for credential management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the my-Blob blog system. Include prerequisites (Java 17+, Node.js, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch), step-by-step installation instructions, database setup with schema and initial data, environment configuration, and running both backend and frontend applications. Document the development server startup process, port configurations, and initial admin account setup. Provide troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Include screenshots and command-line examples for clarity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for the my-Blob blog management system. Document the backend stack including Spring Boot 3.2.5, Java 17+, MyBatis ORM, MySQL database, Redis caching, Elasticsearch search, and JWT authentication. Cover the frontend stack with Vue.js 3.4.21, Vue Router 4.3.0, Pinia 2.1.7, Axios 1.6.8, and WangEditor 5.1.23. Explain the purpose and benefits of each technology choice, version compatibility requirements, and integration patterns between frontend and backend components. Include architectural rationale for technology selections and their role in delivering the blog management functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: MVC Architecture and Layered Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MVC architecture implementation with layered design patterns. Explain the separation between controllers, services, and repositories, documenting how each layer handles responsibilities and interacts with other layers. Detail the controller layer with AdminController and PublicController implementations, service layer with business logic encapsulation, and repository layer with data access patterns. Include concrete examples of request handling flow, service method implementations, and repository operations. Document the integration between layers and how they work together to process requests and manage data.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for file upload endpoints. Document the POST /api/admin/upload endpoint including multipart/form-data request format, supported file types, file size limitations, and upload path configuration. Detail the response schema containing uploaded file URL and error handling for failed uploads. Include practical examples of file upload requests with curl commands, file validation rules, and security considerations for file uploads. Document the upload directory structure and file naming conventions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System and UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Vue.js component system and UI components. Document the component hierarchy starting from Layout.vue as the main container, followed by admin components (Dashboard, Articles, ArticleEdit, Categories, Outlines, Login) and public blog components (Home, Article). Explain component composition patterns, props and events communication, slot usage, and component lifecycle management. Include styling approaches, responsive design implementation, and dark theme support. Document component reusability patterns, prop validation, and event handling. Address component testing strategies and performance optimization techniques for Vue components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the admin dashboard component. Describe the dashboard layout, statistics cards, chart implementations, and real-time data visualization. Document the component's data fetching mechanisms, state management integration, and responsive grid layout. Explain the dashboard metrics presentation, data aggregation patterns, and user interaction elements. Include examples of dashboard card components, data formatting, and real-time updates. Address the dashboard's role in providing admin overview functionality and integration with backend analytics endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer and Business Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the service layer and business logic implementation. Explain how services encapsulate business rules, coordinate between different components, and handle transaction management. Document the service methods for admin authentication, content management operations, and data validation. Detail the service-to-repository interaction patterns, error handling strategies, and business rule enforcement. Include examples of service method implementations, parameter validation, exception handling, and response formatting. Address service layer best practices, dependency injection patterns, and integration with external systems like Redis and Elasticsearch.

wiki_catalogs.name: Article Service - Content Management Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Article Service handling content management operations. Explain the service's role in managing article CRUD operations, content validation, and integration with Elasticsearch for search functionality. Document the service methods for article retrieval, creation, updating, and deletion. Detail the interaction with ArticleMapper for database operations and ArticleSearchRepository for search indexing. Include examples of article publishing workflows, content formatting, search integration patterns, and error handling strategies. Address performance considerations for large content management and search optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Spring Boot backend system. Describe the high-level design using layered architecture with MVC pattern, component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns. Explain technical decisions including MyBatis ORM configuration, repository pattern implementation, service layer architecture, and JWT interceptor setup. Document system boundaries between admin and public APIs, caching strategy with Redis, and security configuration. Include infrastructure requirements, CORS setup, and application initialization. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, and request validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system architecture documentation for the my-Blob blog management system. Describe the high-level design showing the separation between admin panel (backend REST APIs + Vue.js frontend) and public blog interface. Document the layered architecture with MVC pattern in backend and component-based architecture in frontend. Explain the integration points including JWT authentication flow, file upload handling, database operations, and search functionality. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions, data flows between admin and public interfaces, and external service integrations (MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch). Address cross-cutting concerns like security, caching, and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design and ORM Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the blog database schema and MyBatis ORM configuration. Detail entity relationships between Category, Outline, Article, and Admin entities, explaining field definitions, data types, and constraints. Document primary/foreign key relationships, indexes, and database constraints. Explain MyBatis mapper configuration, XML mapping files, and SQL query implementations. Include entity class annotations, table schema definitions, and sample data structures. Document data access patterns, caching strategies, and performance considerations for database operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Category Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for category management CRUD operations. Document POST /api/admin/categories for creating categories with Category entity schema, PUT /api/admin/categories/{id} for updating categories with path parameter and Category body, and DELETE /api/admin/categories/{id} for removing categories. Include request/response schemas, validation rules, error handling for duplicate categories, and cascading effects on outlines and articles. Provide practical examples of category operations with curl commands and JSON payloads.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management with Pinia
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Pinia state management implementation. Detail the authentication store (auth.js) including state properties, actions for login/logout, getters for computed state, and store composition patterns. Explain store modules organization, state persistence strategies, and integration with Vue components. Document reactive state updates, store subscriptions, and cross-component state sharing. Include best practices for store organization, action composition, and state normalization. Address store testing, debugging techniques, and performance considerations for state management in Vue applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Articles Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the admin articles management system. Describe the articles list interface, article creation/editing workflow, and bulk operations functionality. Document the Articles component's data table implementation, filtering and sorting capabilities, and pagination handling. Explain the ArticleEdit component's rich text editing interface, form validation, and save/cancel operations. Include the integration with admin API services, file upload handling, and real-time article status updates. Address the component's state management, form handling patterns, and user experience design for content management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Repository Layer and Data Access
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the repository layer and data access patterns. Detail the MyBatis mapper implementations, SQL query mappings, and database interaction strategies. Explain the repository responsibilities including data retrieval, persistence operations, and query optimization. Document the integration between mappers and entities, result mapping configurations, and dynamic SQL generation. Include examples of CRUD operations, complex queries, batch processing, and search functionality. Address repository best practices, performance optimization techniques, and database connection management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Category Service - Hierarchical Content Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Category Service managing hierarchical content organization. Detail the service's functionality for category CRUD operations, sorting mechanisms, and category hierarchy maintenance. Explain the service methods for category creation, modification, deletion, and retrieval with proper ordering support. Document the integration with CategoryMapper for database operations and any business rules for category validation. Include examples of category management workflows, sorting algorithms, and data consistency maintenance. Address category relationships, validation rules, and performance considerations for category-heavy applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the blog database schema. Detail entity relationships between categories, outlines, articles, and admin users, field definitions, and data types. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain data validation rules and business rules including category sorting, outline hierarchies, and article publishing workflows. Include database schema diagrams showing relationships and sample data from initialization scripts. Document data access patterns through MyBatis mappers, caching strategies with Redis, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle, content management workflows, and search indexing with Elasticsearch.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed core features documentation for the my-Blob blog management system. Document the admin panel functionality including content management (categories, outlines, articles), file upload system, and dashboard analytics. Explain the public blog interface features including category browsing, article reading, search functionality, and responsive design. Detail the authentication system with JWT-based admin login and session management. Include workflow descriptions for admin content creation and publishing process, as well as public user browsing experience. Provide practical examples of feature usage and integration patterns between admin and public interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the JWT-based authentication and security system. Explain JWT token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms implemented in JwtUtil. Document the JwtInterceptor configuration for request authorization, CORS setup, and security header configuration. Detail the authentication flow from login to token validation, including password hashing, token expiration handling, and security best practices. Include practical examples of JWT token usage, interceptor configuration, and security middleware implementation. Address common security considerations and potential vulnerabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Outline Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for outline management CRUD operations. Document POST /api/admin/outlines for creating outlines with Outline entity schema including category_id foreign key relationship, PUT /api/admin/outlines/{id} for updating outlines, and DELETE /api/admin/outlines/{id} for removing outlines. Include hierarchical organization considerations, outline ordering within categories, and validation rules for category relationships. Provide practical examples of outline operations with curl commands and JSON payloads showing category associations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Vue Router configuration and navigation setup. Document the router instance creation, route definitions for admin panel (/admin/*) and public routes (/), navigation guards for authentication, and route parameters handling. Explain programmatic navigation, route meta properties, and dynamic route generation. Include navigation guards implementation, route-based code splitting, and lazy loading strategies. Address route transitions, scroll restoration, and SEO considerations. Document integration with authentication state, protected route handling, and error page management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Categories Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the admin categories management component. Describe the category list interface, category creation and editing forms, and hierarchical category organization. Document the drag-and-drop functionality for category sorting, form validation patterns, and bulk operations. Explain the component's integration with category API endpoints, real-time updates, and error handling. Include examples of category data structures, form field configurations, and user interaction patterns. Address the component's role in content organization and its relationship with outline and article management systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layer Interaction and Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the interaction patterns and data flow between MVC layers. Explain how requests flow from controllers through services to repositories, documenting the complete request processing pipeline. Detail the data transformation between layers, including object mapping, validation, and error propagation. Document the dependency injection patterns, service composition, and transaction boundary management. Include concrete examples of request-response cycles, data validation flows, and error handling across layers. Address performance considerations, logging strategies, and debugging techniques for layered architectures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Outline Service - Content Structure Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Outline Service responsible for content structure management. Explain the service's role in managing outline CRUD operations, hierarchical content organization, and relationship with categories. Document the service methods for outline creation, modification, deletion, and retrieval with proper sorting and ordering support. Detail the integration with OutlineMapper for database operations and the business logic for maintaining outline hierarchies. Include examples of outline management workflows, category-outline relationships, and data integrity maintenance. Address outline validation rules, sorting mechanisms, and performance optimization for complex content structures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the JWT-based authentication and security system. Explain the authentication flow from login to token generation and validation, JWT token structure and claims, password hashing and verification processes, and session management. Document the JwtInterceptor implementation for request authorization, CORS configuration, and security headers. Include practical examples of login requests, token usage in admin API calls, and security best practices. Address common security considerations, token expiration handling, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Provide troubleshooting guidance for authentication issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Environment Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for application configuration and environment management. Document the application.yml configuration structure including database connections, Redis settings, Elasticsearch configuration, and JWT properties. Explain environment-specific configurations, property binding, and externalized configuration management. Detail Redis configuration for caching, DataInitializer for database seeding, and other configuration classes. Include practical examples of configuration usage, environment variable overrides, and production deployment considerations. Address configuration validation, security considerations for sensitive data, and configuration best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Article Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for article management CRUD operations. Document POST /api/admin/articles for creating articles with Article entity schema including outline_id foreign key relationship, PUT /api/admin/articles/{id} for updating articles with rich text content support, and DELETE /api/admin/articles/{id} for removing articles. Include article publishing workflows, content validation rules, and relationships with outlines and categories. Provide practical examples of article operations with curl commands and JSON payloads showing content structure and metadata.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for API integration patterns and HTTP client implementation. Detail the request.js base configuration including axios setup, interceptors, error handling, and request/response transformation. Document API module organization covering admin endpoints, article management, category operations, and outline management. Explain authentication token handling, request retry mechanisms, and error response processing. Include API service composition, data transformation patterns, and caching strategies. Address API testing approaches, mock data handling, and performance optimization for API calls in Vue applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Outlines Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the admin outlines management component. Describe the outline list interface, outline creation and editing workflows, and hierarchical outline organization. Document the component's integration with category relationships, outline sorting mechanisms, and bulk operations. Explain the form handling for outline data, validation rules, and real-time preview functionality. Include examples of outline data structures, category-outline associations, and user interaction patterns. Address the component's role in content structuring and its impact on the overall blog organization system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the admin REST endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for all admin operations. Cover authentication endpoints (/api/admin/login), file upload endpoints (/api/admin/upload), category management CRUD operations, outline management CRUD operations, and article management CRUD operations. Include detailed parameter specifications, response formats, error handling strategies, and status codes. Provide practical examples of API calls with curl commands and JSON payloads. Document rate limiting considerations, security headers, and integration patterns for the admin interface components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Login Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the admin login component. Describe the authentication form interface, input validation, and login submission workflow. Document the component's integration with authentication store, JWT token handling, and route protection mechanisms. Explain the form validation patterns, error handling, and user feedback systems. Include examples of login form structure, authentication state management, and redirect handling after successful login. Address the component's security considerations, input sanitization, and integration with the backend authentication API endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Vue.js frontend application. Describe the component-based architecture using Vue 3 Composition API, routing configuration with Vue Router, state management with Pinia, and build process with Vite. Document the application structure including main.js entry point, App.vue root component, router configuration, and store management. Explain component hierarchy, API integration patterns, and responsive design implementation. Include infrastructure requirements, build optimization, and development workflow. Address cross-browser compatibility, performance optimization, and debugging techniques for the frontend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the admin interface Vue.js components. Describe the layout structure, dashboard functionality, article management interface, category management interface, outline management interface, and login component. Document component props, events, state management, and user interaction patterns. Include usage examples with code snippets, component composition patterns, and integration with admin API services. Provide guidelines for component customization, responsive design implementation, and accessibility compliance. Address component lifecycle, data binding, form handling, and real-time updates. Document the dark theme implementation and overall user experience design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Public Blog Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the public blog interface Vue.js components. Describe the home page component with category listings and article previews, article view component with rich content display, and search functionality implementation. Document component props, data fetching patterns, and user interaction flows. Include usage examples with code snippets, API integration patterns, and responsive design implementation. Provide guidelines for content presentation, navigation patterns, and user experience optimization. Address component composition, data loading strategies, and performance considerations for public content delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Environment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for application configuration and environment setup. Document the Spring Boot application.yml configuration including database connections, Redis settings, Elasticsearch integration, JWT properties, and file upload configurations. Explain Maven build configuration, dependency management, and packaging options. Cover frontend package.json dependencies, build scripts, and Vite configuration. Include environment-specific settings, production deployment configurations, and security considerations. Provide examples of configuration variations for different environments and troubleshooting guidance for common configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development & Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for development workflow and production deployment. Document the development environment setup, hot reload configuration, and debugging techniques for both backend and frontend. Explain the build process for Vue.js application with Vite, Spring Boot packaging, and artifact generation. Cover production deployment strategies including Docker containerization, environment configuration, database migration, and reverse proxy setup. Include performance optimization techniques, monitoring setup, logging configuration, and maintenance procedures. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues and rollback procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation and frequently asked questions for the my-Blob blog system. Document common issues including authentication problems, database connectivity errors, file upload failures, and CORS configuration issues. Provide step-by-step debugging procedures, log analysis techniques, and error resolution strategies. Include FAQ covering typical user questions about content management, search functionality, and system limitations. Address performance troubleshooting, memory optimization, and scaling considerations. Provide diagnostic commands, configuration validation steps, and escalation procedures for complex issues.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Blog Management System - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
This is a full-stack blog management system designed to provide both public blog viewing capabilities and administrative content management functionality. The system supports content creation, categorization, and publication through an intuitive admin interface while offering a clean reading experience for end users.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Content Management**: Enable administrators to create, edit, and manage blog content through a dedicated admin panel
- **User Experience**: Provide a responsive and visually appealing blog reading experience for visitors
- **Scalability**: Support multiple content types including categories, outlines, and articles
- **Security**: Implement JWT-based authentication for admin access control
- **Performance**: Utilize caching and modern technologies for optimal performance

### Target Audience
- **Administrators**: Content creators and managers who need to maintain blog content
- **End Users**: Visitors seeking to read published blog articles
- **Developers**: Maintainers working with the Spring Boot and Vue.js stack

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        FE[Vue.js Frontend]
        Router[Vue Router]
        Store[Pinia State Management]
        Components[Vue Components]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Layer"
        BE[Spring Boot Backend]
        Controller[REST Controllers]
        Service[Business Services]
        Repository[Data Access Layer]
        Entity[Domain Models]
    end
    
    subgraph "Infrastructure"
        DB[(MySQL Database)]
        Cache[(Redis Cache)]
        ES[(Elasticsearch)]
        Storage[(File Storage)]
    end
    
    FE --> BE
    Router --> Components
    Store --> Components
    Components --> Controller
    Controller --> Service
    Service --> Repository
    Repository --> Entity
    Entity --> DB
    Service --> Cache
    Service --> ES
    Controller --> Storage
```

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access through mapper interfaces
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulation
- **JWT Authentication**: Stateless authentication mechanism
- **CQRS-like Approach**: Separate admin and public endpoints

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant Service
    participant Database
    
    User->>Frontend: Access Admin Panel
    Frontend->>Backend: POST /api/admin/login
    Backend->>Service: Authenticate Credentials
    Service->>Database: Verify User
    Database-->>Service: User Data
    Service-->>Backend: Authenticated User
    Backend-->>Frontend: JWT Token
    Frontend->>Backend: Authorized Requests
    Backend->>Service: Process Admin Actions
    Service->>Database: CRUD Operations
    Database-->>Service: Results
    Service-->>Backend: Response
    Backend-->>Frontend: JSON Response
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Backend**: `blog-backend/src/main/java/com/blog/BlogApplication.java` - Spring Boot application startup
- **Frontend**: `blog-frontend/src/main.js` - Vue.js application initialization

### Core Modules

#### Backend Architecture
```mermaid
classDiagram
    class BlogApplication {
        +main(args)
    }
    
    class AdminController {
        +login(credentials)
        +uploadImage(file)
        +createCategory(category)
        +updateCategory(id, category)
        +deleteCategory(id)
        +createOutline(outline)
        +updateOutline(id, outline)
        +deleteOutline(id)
        +createArticle(article)
        +updateArticle(id, article)
        +deleteArticle(id)
    }
    
    class WebConfig {
        +addInterceptors()
        +addResourceHandlers()
        +addCorsMappings()
    }
    
    class JwtInterceptor {
        +preHandle()
    }
    
    BlogApplication --> AdminController
    WebConfig --> JwtInterceptor
    AdminController --> JwtInterceptor
```

#### Frontend Architecture
```mermaid
graph LR
    App[App.vue] --> Router[Router Config]
    Router --> Views[View Components]
    Views --> Admin[Admin Views]
    Views --> Public[Public Views]
    
    Admin --> Layout[Layout.vue]
    Admin --> Dashboard[Dashboard.vue]
    Admin --> Articles[Articles.vue]
    Admin --> Categories[Categories.vue]
    Admin --> Outlines[Outlines.vue]
    
    Public --> Home[Home.vue]
    Public --> Article[Article.vue]
    
    Views --> API[API Layer]
    API --> Request[HTTP Client]
    API --> Stores[State Management]
```

### Configuration Approach
- **Backend**: `blog-backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` - Centralized configuration for database, Redis, Elasticsearch, and JWT settings
- **Frontend**: `blog-frontend/package.json` - Dependency management and build scripts
- **Build Configuration**: `blog-backend/pom.xml` - Maven build configuration with Spring Boot parent

### External Dependencies
- **Backend**: Spring Boot 3.2.5, MyBatis, MySQL Connector, Redis, Elasticsearch, JWT libraries
- **Frontend**: Vue 3.4.21, Vue Router 4.3.0, Pinia 2.1.7, Axios 1.6.8, WangEditor 5.1.23

### Integration Points
- **Authentication**: JWT-based token authentication with interceptor-based authorization
- **File Upload**: Direct file handling with configurable upload paths
- **Caching**: Redis-based caching for improved performance
- **Search**: Elasticsearch integration for content search capabilities

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph "Data Flow"
        A[Admin Login] --> B[JWT Token Generation]
        B --> C[Authorized Requests]
        C --> D[CRUD Operations]
        D --> E[Database Operations]
    end
    
    subgraph "State Management"
        F[Local Storage] --> G[Pinia Store]
        G --> H[Vue Components]
        H --> I[API Calls]
    end
    
    subgraph "Caching Strategy"
        J[Redis Cache] --> K[Service Layer]
        K --> L[Database Cache]
    end
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Admin Authentication**: Secure login system with JWT token management
- **Content Management**: Complete CRUD operations for categories, outlines, and articles
- **File Upload**: Image upload functionality with automatic file naming
- **Responsive Design**: Modern UI with dark theme aesthetic
- **Real-time Statistics**: Dashboard showing content statistics

### Implementation Highlights

#### Admin Interface (`blog-frontend/src/views/admin/`)
- **Dashboard**: Real-time content statistics and overview
- **Articles Management**: Full CRUD operations with rich text editing
- **Categories Management**: Organizational structure management
- **Outlines Management**: Hierarchical content organization

#### Public Blog Interface (`blog-frontend/src/views/`)
- **Home Page**: Featured content and recent articles
- **Article View**: Optimized reading experience
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with gradient backgrounds

#### Backend API (`blog-backend/src/main/java/com/blog/controller/`)
- **RESTful Endpoints**: Clean API design with proper HTTP methods
- **Validation**: Input validation and error handling
- **Security**: JWT-based authentication and authorization

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Login: Admin Access
    Login --> Authenticated: Valid Credentials
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: Successful Login
    Authenticated --> Articles: Manage Content
    Authenticated --> Categories: Manage Structure
    Authenticated --> Outlines: Organize Content
    Authenticated --> Logout: Session End
    Logout --> Unauthenticated: Token Expired
    Dashboard --> Authenticated: Navigation
    Articles --> Authenticated: Navigation
    Categories --> Authenticated: Navigation
    Outlines --> Authenticated: Navigation
```

### Performance Considerations
- **Caching Strategy**: Redis-based caching for frequently accessed data
- **Lazy Loading**: Vue.js dynamic imports for route-based component loading
- **Optimized Queries**: MyBatis mapper optimization for database operations
- **Asset Optimization**: CDN-ready file upload and serving infrastructure

---

**Sources:**

- [AdminController.java](blog-backend/src/main/java/com/blog/controller/AdminController.java)
- [WebConfig.java](blog-backend/src/main/java/com/blog/config/WebConfig.java)
- [RedisConfig.java](blog-backend/src/main/java/com/blog/config/RedisConfig.java)
- [application.yml](blog-backend/src/main/resources/application.yml)

- [Category.java](blog-backend/src/main/java/com/blog/entity/Category.java)
- [package.json](blog-frontend/package.json)

</blog>
